title: N-body Lie Algebra Dimension Scaling Formulas

L2_original.note: Fitted from N=3,4,5,6 (4 points determine unique cubic). Predicts 477 for N=7 but observed 476; predicts 752 for N=8 but observed 748. Failure caused by N=3 boundary effect.

L2_corrected.boundary_note: N=3 gives new_L2=11 (not 12*C(3,3)=12). The formula L2(N) = N(4N^2-9N+3)/2 holds for N>=4. For N=3 the cumulative is 17, matching C(3,2) + N(N-2) + 11 = 3+3+11.
L2_corrected.graph_theoretic_conjecture: new_L2(N) = 12*C(N,3) counts the number of oriented triangles in the complete graph K_N times a symmetry factor

L3.note: Two data points available (N=3,4). Not enough to determine functional form; L3(N) requires at least N=5 level 3 data.

observations.degree_pattern: L0 ~ N^2, L1 ~ N^2, L2 ~ N^3. Leading polynomial degree increases with level.
observations.super_polynomial_growth: For fixed N, growth across levels is super-polynomial: L3/L2 = 6.82 for N=3, L4/L3 = 19.4 for N=3.
observations.graph_theory_connection: new_L2(N) = 12*C(N,3) suggests bracket dimensions count combinatorial structures on the interaction graph K_N.
